Title: Sirāt
Author: Unknown
Summary: "Sirāt" is an R-rated suspense drama set in Morocco, directed by Oliver Laxe. The 115-minute film, starring Sergi López and others, follows a father and son searching for their missing daughter at a mountain rave. It releases in Los Angeles and New York on November 14, 2025, with a wider release on March 6, 2026.
Content:
Sirāt is a drama film set in a foreign suspense genre, rated R, with a runtime of 115 minutes. It is scheduled for release on November 14, 2025, in Los Angeles and New York, and on March 6, 2026. Directed by Oliver Laxe and written by Santiago Fillol and Oliver Laxe, the cast includes Sergi López, Bruno Núñez, Stefania Gadda, Joshua Liam Henderson, Tonin Janvier, Jade Oukid, and Richard Bellamy. 

The story follows a father and his son who arrive at a rave deep in the mountains of southern Morocco. They are searching for Mar, their daughter and sister, who vanished months earlier at one of these endless, sleepless parties. Surrounded by electronic music and a raw, unfamiliar sense of freedom, they repeatedly display her photo, holding onto hope as they follow a group of ravers to one last destination.

Title: The Bride
Author: Unknown
Summary: "The Bride" (2026), directed and written by Maggie Gyllenhaal, is an R-rated horror-drama-romance film set for release on March 6, 2026. Starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale, and others, it follows a lonely Frankenstein (Bale) who revives a murdered woman, creating The Bride. The film explores themes of love, macabre romance, and ambitious ideas.
Content:
The Bride (2026) is a horror, drama, and romance film rated R, with a runtime of 126 minutes. It is set to be released on March 6, 2026, and directed by Maggie Gyllenhaal, who also wrote the screenplay. The cast includes Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale, Peter Sarsgaard, Annette Bening, Jake Gyllenhaal, and Penélope Cruz. The studio behind the film is Warner Brothers.

The story follows a lonely Frankenstein (Bale) who travels to 1930s Chicago to meet groundbreaking scientist Dr. Euphronious (Annette Bening) to create a companion. Together, they revive a murdered young woman, resulting in the birth of The Bride (Buckley).

The film is described as a bold, iconoclastic take on a compelling story, blending life, macabre romance, and ambitious ideas. Maggie Gyllenhaal's vision presents a beautiful yet hideous creature that is both captivating and unsettling.

Title: Hoppers
Author: Unknown
Summary: "Hoppers" is a 2026 animated sci-fi comedy film directed by Daniel Chong, featuring Jon Hamm, Bobby Moynihan, and Piper Curda. Produced by Walt Disney, it releases on March 6, 2026. The story follows Mabel as she explores a world where human consciousness can be transferred into robotic animals, uncovering hidden mysteries.
Content:
Hoppers (2026) is an action animated comedy sci-fi film rated PG, with a runtime of 105 minutes. It is set to be released on March 6, 2026. The film is directed by Daniel Chong and written by Jesse Andrews and Daniel Chong. The cast includes Jon Hamm, Bobby Moynihan, and Piper Curda. It is produced by Walt Disney Pictures.

In “Hoppers,” scientists have discovered how to “hop” human consciousness into lifelike robotic animals, allowing people to communicate with animals as animals. Using this new technology, Mabel (Curda) will uncover mysteries within the animal world that are beyond anything she could have imagined.

Title: How to Make a Killing
Author: Unknown
Summary: "How to Make a Killing" (2026) is a crime suspense comedy directed by John Patton Ford, starring Glen Powell and Margaret Qualley. Produced by A24, it follows Becket Redfellow, a wealthy man's disowned son, who plans to kill relatives to inherit $28 billion. The 105-minute R-rated film features crime, violence, and dark humor.
Content:
How to Make a Killing (2026) is a crime suspense comedy directed by John Patton Ford, who also wrote the screenplay. The film stars Glen Powell, Margaret Qualley, Jessica Henwick, Bill Camp, Zach Woods, Topher Grace, and Ed Harris. It is produced by A24 and has a runtime of 105 minutes. The movie is rated R for language and some violence/bloody images.

Disowned at birth by his obscenely wealthy family, blue-collar Becket Redfellow (Glen Powell) will stop at nothing to reclaim his inheritance, no matter how many relatives stand in his way.

In the film, Glen Powell plays a man who has not been dealt much luck in life: he’s incredibly good-looking, has a high-earning career, and is admired by two women. However, he considers killing off several relatives to inherit $28 billion.

Title: Traffic Collison - Circle Dr E/Highway 11 - Update
Author: Unknown
Summary: Saskatoon police report that one eastbound lane on Circle Drive and one northbound lane on Highway 11 are open after a collision, with no injuries. Traffic restrictions remain, and a semi carrying equipment caused damage to Highway 11 and the bridge, leading to closures for assessment. Motorists are advised to take alternate routes.
Content:
The Saskatoon Police Service advises that City of Saskatoon engineers have assessed the damage, and one eastbound lane on Circle Drive and one northbound lane of Highway 11 have been opened for traffic to proceed. The Saskatoon Police Service Collision Analyst Unit, in cooperation with the Saskatchewan Highway Patrol, is investigating the collision. No injuries were reported.

Traffic flow restrictions will remain until further notice. Motorists are asked to take alternate routes or expect delays in the area.

The Saskatoon Police Service also reports that a semi with trailer carrying a piece of equipment in the northbound lane of Highway 11 from Regina entering the city limits at the Circle Drive overpass was too high for the structure, causing significant damage. Highway 11 northbound lanes into the city limits and Circle Drive eastbound lanes over the bridge will be closed until engineers from the City of Saskatoon assess the damage and determine if it is safe for travel. Motorists are advised to take alternate routes until further notice.

Title: The Inertia v3 Beta is Here
Author: Paul Redmond
Summary: The Inertia v3 Beta introduces a built-in XHR HTTP client, making Axios optional, and adds a use Httphook for standalone HTTP requests. It expands optimistic updates with support for concurrent changes and instant navigation for faster user experience. A new server-side preserve Fragment option retains URL hash fragments across redirects, enhancing navigation consistency.
Content:
The Inertia v3 Beta is a major pre-release milestone that introduces several new features and improvements. It now includes a built-in XHR HTTP client, making Axios an optional peer dependency. Applications that do not require Axios can omit it entirely, while those that prefer it can continue using it by installing it as a peer dependency.

A new use Httphook has been added to facilitate standalone HTTP requests outside of Inertia, providing a similar developer experience to use Form, including processing, errors, progress, and dirtystate. This hook returns response data instead of triggering page navigation and supports surfacing all validation errors.

Optimistic updates have been expanded across the router, use Form, and use Http, with support for concurrent updates. The update callback can apply immediate optimistic state changes, with automatic rollback on validation errors, server errors, or interrupted requests. Both fluent API and inline options are supported.

Instant support has been introduced to make navigation feel immediate by rendering the destination page before the server response completes.

Additionally, a new server-side preserve Fragment option allows the URL hash fragment to remain intact across redirects. In Laravel, this can be achieved by chaining preserve Fragment() on a redirect response. When the server includes preserve Fragment: true in the Inertia page object, the client retains the original URL fragment even during redirects.

Title: He Had a Purple Heart, PTSD and a Rap Sheet. He Had to Leave the U.S.
Author: Max Kim
Summary: Sae Joon Park, a U.S. Army veteran and deportee, faces potential deafness amid health issues in South Korea. Having been deported last year due to a criminal record, he struggles with language barriers, as he hasn't lived in South Korea since age 7. His case has garnered media attention and a congressional hearing, with promises of review by officials.
Content:
Sae Joon Park’s life was in pieces after he was swept up in the Trump administration’s deportation push last year. But on a recent Tuesday, he was preoccupied with the more immediate concern that he might be going deaf.

At a walk-in clinic in Busan, South Korea, he explained to the receptionist, in slightly accented Korean, how his left eardrum had inexplicably ruptured in his sleep a few weeks earlier. Now his right ear was fading out, too.

“Fill in the blanks, please,” she said, producing an intake form.

“I can’t read or write Korean,” Mr. Park said.

It was a line he had delivered many times in recent weeks, explained by the fact that he hadn’t lived in South Korea since he was 7, when he immigrated to the United States. A U.S. Army veteran with combat trauma and a Purple Heart, he was ordered to leave America at the age of 56 over a criminal history that he thought he had atoned for.

He had pleaded his case in nearly two dozen media interviews. In December, he video-called into a congressional hearing where former Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem promised to look into his case.

Title: Human Strategy In An AI-Accelerated Workflow
Author: hello@smashingmagazine.com (Carrie Webster)
Summary: UX design is evolving as AI rapidly automates tasks like wireframes and prototypes, raising fears of job displacement. However, UX remains focused on navigating ambiguity, advocating for users, and solving complex human problems. While AI can handle artifact creation, the core of UX—understanding and addressing human needs—continues to require human insight and judgment.
Content:
UX design is entering a new phase, with designers shifting from makers of outputs to directors of intent. AI can now generate wireframes, prototypes, and even design systems in minutes, but UX has never been only about creating interfaces. It’s about navigating ambiguity, advocating for humans in systems optimized for efficiency, and solving their problems through thoughtful design.

I’ve been working in User Experience design for more than twenty years. Long enough to have held many job titles, from when stakeholders asked us to “just make it pretty” to when wireframes were delivered as annotated PDFs. I’ve seen many tools come and go over the years, methodologies rise and fall, and entire platforms disappear. Yet, nothing has unsettled designers quite like AI.

When generative AI tools first entered my workflow, my reaction wasn’t excitement — it was unease, with a little curiosity. Watching an interface appear in seconds, complete with sensible spacing, readable typography, and halfway-decent copy, triggered a very real fear: if a machine can do this, where does that leave me? That fear is now widespread. Designers at every level ask the same question, often quietly: “Will an AI agent replace me by next week, month, or year?”

While the difference between next week and next year is significant, it depends on where you are in your career and how quickly your employer chooses to adopt AI tools. I have been fortunate in several roles to work with organizations that haven’t allowed the use of AI due to data security concerns. For those interested, discussions about AI’s impact are ongoing on platforms like Reddit.

Fearing the takeover of AI in our roles is not irrational. We’re AI generating wireframes, prototypes, personas, usability summaries, accessibility suggestions, and entire design systems. Tasks that once took days can now be completed in minutes. Here’s the uncomfortable truth: if your role is largely about producing artifacts, drawing buttons, aligning components, or translating instructions into screens, then parts of that work are already being automated.

Still, UX design has never truly been about just creating a user interface. It’s about navigating ambiguity. It’s about advocating for humans in systems optimized for efficiency. It’s about translating messy human needs into effective solutions.

Title: Lebanon at ‘Tipping Point’ as It Seeks to Disarm Hezbollah
Author: Christina Goldbaum
Summary: Lebanon's government, balancing U.S. and regional pressures, faces a critical moment amid escalating Israel-Hezbollah conflict. Israeli strikes in Beirut displaced thousands, raising fears of civil war. Experts warn this could lead to clashes between Lebanese forces and Hezbollah or disarmament, as tensions intensify following Hezbollah's retaliation for Iran's leader's death.
Content:
For much of the past year, Lebanon’s government has navigated a delicate balance in its dealings with Hezbollah, the Iranian-backed armed group, as it has sought to disarm militants and reduce their influence in Lebanese politics. Now, with Lebanon facing a rapidly escalating conflict between Hezbollah and Israel, the country is awaiting whether the government will seize this moment to take decisive action against Hezbollah and how the group will respond.

Overnight, the Israeli military launched strikes against Hezbollah in the southern outskirts of Beirut, with explosions audible across the city. The bombardment displaced thousands of residents from the densely populated area, many of whom spent the night on the streets of downtown Beirut.

“This is the tipping point,” said Sami Nader, director of the Institute of Political Science at Saint Joseph University of Beirut. “Either we have the dark scenario where the army clashes with Hezbollah and there is civil strife, or Hezbollah abides by the government’s decision and they disarm.”

Lebanese officials have had to maintain a careful balance over the past year: appeasing demands from the United States and other allies for swift action against Hezbollah while proceeding cautiously to avoid clashes between Lebanese soldiers and militants, a scenario that many fear could trigger civil conflict.

However, when Hezbollah began firing at Israel this week in retaliation for the killing of Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, igniting more intense fighting, that careful calculation appeared to unravel.

Title: 'This is wrong,' Lebanese resident says as hundreds of thousands displaced amid Israeli strikes
Summary: Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon have killed at least 217 people and displaced hundreds of thousands, forcing many to sleep outdoors. The conflict, involving Hezbollah and Iran-backed militias, has intensified since Israel and the U.S. targeted Iran. Residents fear for safety amid ongoing bombings, with Israel ordering Lebanese civilians to evacuate southern Beirut and parts of the Bekaa Valley.
Content:
Israeli airstrikes continued to pound Lebanon on Friday, forcing hundreds of thousands to flee their homes in densely populated neighborhoods to shelter elsewhere, as aid groups warned the escalating conflict could lead to a humanitarian catastrophe. According to Lebanon's Health Ministry, at least 217 people have been killed and hundreds more injured since the conflict intensified on Monday.

Displaced people fleeing Israeli airstrikes in the Lebanese capital's southern suburb of Dahiyeh were seen sleeping on the Beirut corniche, some in cars, others on the street or the beach. Jamal Seifeddin, 43, who spent the night outside in downtown Beirut, described the dire conditions, saying, "We’re sleeping here in the streets — some in cars, some on the street, some on the beach. No one even brought a blanket."

The southern suburbs of Beirut were again targeted by Israeli airstrikes as Israel traded blows with the Iranian-backed militia Hezbollah. Following U.S. and Israeli attacks against Iran that began a week ago, Hezbollah launched missiles and drones into Israel on Monday for the first time in over a year. Israel responded with bombardments of southern Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs.

Residents fleeing the area expressed fears for their safety. Hadi Kaakour, a resident fleeing Beirut's southern suburbs, said he was unsure he would be safe anywhere, stating, "We don't put anything past [Israel], they will strike us no matter where we go." Others voiced frustration at Lebanon being pulled into the conflict, with Yousef Nabulsi saying, "We got sucked into a mess that we have nothing to do with. People have been displaced and are now staying on the streets, and this is wrong."

Israel's military ordered Lebanese residents out of Beirut's southern suburbs, including areas controlled by Hezbollah, as well as parts of the eastern Bekaa Valley, after previously ordering people out of a section of south Lebanon.

Title: Justice Department releases Epstein files that include uncorroborated Trump allegation, after complaints
Author: Chris Iorfida
Summary: The U.S. Justice Department released previously undisclosed files related to Jeffrey Epstein, including allegations from a woman claiming she was raped by Epstein's associate "Jeff" in the 1980s and made claims involving Donald Trump, which the White House dismissed as baseless. The files, part of the Epstein Transparency Act, faced scrutiny over their handling and include unverified and sensational claims.
Content:
The U.S. Justice Department has released additional files connected to the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, including descriptions of interviews with a woman making allegations against President Donald Trump, a onetime Epstein friend.

The department said Thursday the files had been "incorrectly coded as duplicative," and therefore not originally published. The department has faced scrutiny in Congress over its handling of the documents, which it is required to make public as part of the Epstein Transparency Act, which was spurred by efforts from both Democratic and Republican legislators.

Some of the new records pertain to a woman who contacted the FBI shortly after Epstein’s 2019 arrest and claimed that a man named “Jeff” living in Hilton Head, S.C., had raped her there in the 1980s when she was around 13 years old. The woman told the agents she didn’t know the man’s identity at the time, but decades later concluded he was Jeffrey Epstein when a friend texted her his photo from a news story.

The woman was interviewed four times by FBI agents and over time added a host of other claims, including that Epstein had schemed to have her mother sent to prison, beaten her, arranged sexual encounters with other men, and once flew her to either New Jersey or New York sometime in the mid-'80s, where she claimed to have bitten Trump after he tried to sexually assault her. She eventually declined to answer additional questions and broke off contact with agents, according to the files.

White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt called the woman's claims "completely baseless accusations, backed by zero credible evidence." The Justice Department has cautioned that some of the documents include "untrue and sensationalist claims" against Trump.

It is not precisely known when the Epstein and Trump relationship began, but the earliest period that has been publicly suggested is the late 1980s.

Title: Carney approval high in Sask., Liberals at 49 per cent national voter support: poll
Author: Postmedia News
Summary: A Postmedia-Leger poll shows high approval for Mark Carney in Western provinces, with 53% in Alberta and 59% in Manitoba and Saskatchewan. 59% of Canadians are satisfied with Prime Minister Carney’s government, while support for the Bloc Québécois and NDP remains low. Conservative support has declined slightly, and most Canadians prefer waiting until 2029 for the next election.
Content:
Western provinces showed high approval ratings for Mark Carney, at 53 per cent for Alberta and 59 per cent for Manitoba and Saskatchewan, according to a new Postmedia-Leger poll. Leger executive vice-president Andrew Enns noted Saskatchewan Premier Scott Moe has taken two international trips with Carney and has come back with results for his constituents.

High approval ratings in Saskatchewan for Prime Minister Mark Carney demonstrate what happens when a federal leader doesn’t go out of their way to pick fights with certain regions of the country, the executive vice-president of a national pollster said. The poll also showed support for the Bloc Québécois falling to five per cent, compared to six per cent in January. Support for the New Democratic Party remained stable at five per cent.

The poll indicated that 59 per cent of respondents are satisfied with the performance of the Liberal government led by Carney. “We’re actually a full year in of having Prime Minister Carney managing the affairs of the government,” Enns said. “They’ve done a very good job of keeping government activity, government message, on track in terms of what Canadians are expecting of their government during these times.”

Enns noted that the poll is “not good news” for the Conservatives, whose support has remained around 37 to 38 per cent over the last 18 months. “Now suddenly they have lost some support,” said Enns. “I think it probably should be concerning to them.” He suggested this could be due to their old messaging falling flat or possibly related to Carney’s personal approval ratings. Some conservative-minded voters are responding positively to Carney.

Speculation has persisted over the past few months that Carney may call an early election to secure a majority government, as the Liberals remain strong in the polls. However, about half of Canadians polled said the next election should be in 2029, as set out in the fixed election date legislation. “Canadians don’t want an election now, they quite frankly don’t want one this spring,” Enns said. “And when you like what you’re getting, which 60 per cent of Canadians do—how the government’s performing, how the prime minister is performing—they’re not interested in changing that.”

Nearly 71 per cent of Liberal voters would prefer an election in 2029. Notably, nearly 24 per cent of Conservative voters would like an election now; 19 per cent would like one this spring, and 20 per cent this fall. “I have to just chalk that up to sort of that hard partisanship,” Enns said, “that a certain swath of that Conservative Party, that intensity that they hold, that they’ll take an election whenever they can get it.”

Title: Warm to early sounds of spring: Five things to do this weekend in Saskatoon, March 6-8
Author: Jocelyn Bennett, Saskatoon StarPhoenix
Summary: A women’s march was held at Vimy Memorial in Kiwainis Memorial Park on International Women’s Day, featuring a book reading and musical performances. Upcoming events include a tribute concert to 1950s music on Friday, a brass band concert on Sunday, and Ukrainian comedian Yurii Kolotylyn’s standup shows Saturday at Art Bar. The University of Saskatchewan Huskies women’s basketball team advances to semifinals.
Content:
Commemorating International Women’s Day, a women’s march was held at the Vimy Memorial in Kiwainis Memorial Park on Sunday at 1 p.m. The event included a book reading, a performance of piano pieces, and some favorite cover songs. 

Enjoy a modern take on classic music with "An Evening with the Bromantics," which brings the 1950s to the stage in a tribute to the golden era. The six-piece Saskatchewan band explores Motown and early rock with songs from Roy Orbison, The Platters, Buddy Holly, and Sam Cooke. The all-ages concert is scheduled for Friday at the Broadway Theatre at 8 p.m.

The Bridge City Brass Band presents traditional and contemporary brass band music. The concert features familiar tunes, along with the Saskatoon premiere of "A Gallimaufry Suite" by Philip Harper and "Eternity" by Michael Bojesen. It will be held on Sunday at Grace-Westminster United Church at 2:30 p.m.

The University of Saskatchewan Huskies women's basketball team advances to the semifinal stage.

Saskatoon-based Ukrainian comedian Yurii Kolotylyn is bringing his comedy to the stage. He will perform his full-length standup show in Ukrainian, followed by an English version. The shows, limited to ages 19 and older, are scheduled for Saturday at Art Bar at 7 p.m. in Ukrainian and 9 p.m. in English.

Title: Dillon Brooks, Canadian NBA player with the Phoenix Suns, arrested for suspicion of DUI after traffic stop
Summary: Phoenix Suns forward Dillon Brooks was arrested for suspicion of DUI early Friday in Scottsdale, Arizona, after a traffic stop. He was taken to jail and released around 3:30 a.m. Brooks, 30, has been out since February 21 with a fractured left hand and is expected to return in late March. The Suns are gathering information.
Content:
Phoenix Suns forward Dillon Brooks was arrested for suspicion of driving under the influence early Friday morning in Scottsdale, Arizona, police said. Officers conducted a traffic stop around 1 a.m., and after an investigation, Brooks was taken to jail and released following the booking process around 3:30 a.m.

The 30-year-old from Mississauga, Ontario, has not played since February 21 due to a fractured left hand. He is expected to be out until at least late March after undergoing surgery.

The Suns issued a statement saying, "We are aware of the situation involving Dillon Brooks and are gathering more information. We have no further comment at this time."

The team played the Chicago Bulls at home on Thursday night, just hours before his arrest, losing 105-103. Before his injury, Brooks was having the best season of his NBA career, averaging a career-high 20.9 points per game. He joined the Suns as part of the trade that sent Kevin Durant to the Rockets during the offseason.

Title: 50 Cent Appears to Clap Back at T.I. on New Power Origins Theme
Author: C. Vernon Coleman II
Summary: 50 Cent released a Power Origins theme song with lyrics referencing T.I. and Tiny's sexual assault allegations, which they deny. He also suggested a documentary on the matter. T.I. responded with a defiant Instagram post, taunting 50 Cent and releasing a diss track. The feud between the two continues.
Content:
50 Cent appears to have taken a jab at T.I. and the Harris family on the new Power Origins theme song. On Thursday night (March 5), the G-Unit head honcho shared the track for the upcoming Power TV show universe, featuring Grammy Award-winning singer Leon Thomas. In the television intro, Fif appears to reference T.I. and Tiny with provocative lyrics: "I'm back on my dope boy grammar / Your daddy made your mama eat every box in Atlanta," and "Freak sh*t / Peep sh*t / Keep sh*t, on the low / But everybody know." He captioned the post, "50cent Ft Leon Thomas (No one told us what we’re here for) POWER ORIGINS theme song!"

The lyrics seem to allude to T.I. and Tiny being accused of sexual assault by multiple women, though none of the claims have been proven in court. Both T.I. and Tiny have denied the allegations.

This track followed a since-deleted social media post from 50 Cent on March 4, where he claimed he was working on a "Surviving T.I. and Tiny Documentary." He wrote, "Remember how quiet I got before the DIddy doc, Dame thought I wasn't coming. I hope this doesn't mess up your promo tour—they're gonna ask about your 20 sexual assault cases. You might want to talk to a crisis PR person."

T.I. responded to 50's comments and the song with a defiant message on Instagram: "We can go bar for bar, hit for hit, or doc for doc... either way all you gon do is show how much of a FN u is. Let’s play. Nobody fears you over here Boo Boo…" He also shared a new song titled "Trauma Bond," in which he raps about his family and takes a shot at 50 Cent, writing, "@50cent Drop whatever you choose, just please keep showing da world You a H*!!!" and "#On Clay & You still ain’t Tell us why you in 4K 'D**k Pulling' wit the smile of a Sissy???? Pandora’s box is ajar sir… Good Luck FN You suck. P.S. Me & mine till the end of time Issa 'Trauma Bond'!!!!"

The ongoing feud between T.I. and 50 Cent shows no signs of ending soon.

Title: Israeli troops push into Lebanon for yet another war with Iran's proxy Hezbollah
Author: Unknown
Summary: Israeli troops have entered Lebanon, capturing hilltops near the border amid ongoing clashes with Hezbollah, which has launched rockets and drones at northern Israel. Israel has issued a large-scale evacuation in southern Lebanon, aiming to disarm Hezbollah. The conflict follows recent Israeli airstrikes and a significant military buildup, with fears of a full-scale invasion and increased civilian displacement.
Content:
Israeli troops have entered Lebanon for what appears to be another conflict involving Iran's proxy, Hezbollah. Along the northern border, bursts of machine-gun fire from inside Lebanon are loud and sustained as new Israeli forces push to secure strategic positions near the border. The sound of air strikes echoes over the remains of Shia villages destroyed during Israel's last ground war there in 2024.

A senior military official stated that Israeli ground forces are capturing additional hilltops inside Lebanon as a defensive measure to better protect northern communities. However, there has been a significant military buildup, with dozens of tanks and armored bulldozers recently positioned near the border, fueling speculation of a full-scale invasion.

Israel has issued a large-scale evacuation order for southern Lebanon, extending roughly 27 kilometers (16 miles) inside the country. The Israel Defense Forces Chief of Staff, Eyal Zamir, has said the goal is to disarm Hezbollah and that operations will continue until that is achieved. A senior military official, speaking anonymously, indicated that forces are prepared to move as deep as the Litani River and beyond if instructed, emphasizing plans are in place for immediate action.

Hezbollah, allied with Iran, joined the conflict on Monday and has been launching rockets and drones at northern Israel daily, most of which are intercepted by Israeli air defenses. Residents in one Israeli border community affected by a drone attack on Tuesday expressed reluctance to leave, questioning where they would go given the dangers elsewhere.

Israel's opposition leader, Yair Lapid, suggested creating a "sterile zone" in southern Lebanon, similar to the Yellow Line in Gaza, which marks the boundary between Israeli-controlled territory and Hamas-held areas. He proposed removing Lebanese villages from the zone, arguing that they had become host to terrorist organizations and that such measures, though unpleasant, were justified.

In recent days, hundreds of thousands of Lebanese civilians have been fleeing Israeli air strikes, less than 18 months after a ceasefire ended the previous conflict.

Title: Complex repairs needed after semi collision with Saskatoon overpass
Author: thestarphoenix
Summary: A semi hauling heavy equipment struck the Highway 11, Highway 16, and Circle Drive overpass in Saskatoon, causing significant damage. Traffic was reduced to a single lane, with closures affecting northbound Highway 11 and eastbound Circle Drive. No injuries occurred. Repairs are underway, but the timeline is unclear due to the extent of damage.
Content:
Traffic moved through a single open lane beneath the Circle Drive overpass on Highway 11 as crews assessed damage after a semi hauling heavy equipment struck the structure Thursday afternoon. Saskatoon Police said the collision caused significant damage to the overpass at the Highway 11, Highway 16, and Circle Drive cloverleaf.

Repairs are needed on a busy overpass in Saskatoon after cargo on a semi-trailer collided with the concrete structure. The City of Saskatoon stated that the extent of the damage or the cost of fixing the section of the cloverleaf at Circle Drive and Highway 11 was not immediately known after the collision Thursday afternoon. Planning for repairs was underway on Friday, though a repair timeline was not available due to “the complexity and extent of the required work,” the city said in a news release.

According to Saskatoon police, a semi with a trailer on Thursday around 5 p.m., carrying a piece of equipment in the northbound lane of Highway 11 from Regina entering the city limits at the Circle Drive overpass, was too high for the structure and caused significant damage. Northbound traffic on Highway 11 and eastbound traffic on Circle Drive were immediately closed off for hours before one lane in each direction was reopened. No injuries were reported.

City engineers, Saskatoon police, emergency services, and collision analysts were all on the scene. The analysts were working with Saskatchewan Highway Patrol to investigate. Work began on Friday morning to safely remove loose concrete and address safety concerns. Southbound traffic on Highway 11 and westbound traffic on Highway 16 remained unaffected. Northbound semi trucks on Highway 11 were being detoured around the area at Township 360 via Range Road 3050.

Title: Despite off-night shooting, Huskies advance to women's hoops semifinal
Author: thestarphoenix
Summary: The University of Saskatchewan Huskies advanced to the U Sports women’s basketball semifinals with a 71-55 win over UBC, despite shooting poorly. Led by Gage Grassick’s 25 points, they aim for a second consecutive national title. They will face Laval on Saturday, after Laval upset top-ranked Toronto Metropolitan. Calgary and New Brunswick also advanced to the semifinals.
Content:
Though the University of Saskatchewan Huskies were off-target on the offensive end, they were sharp enough defensively to make up for it as they began their pursuit for back-to-back national basketball titles. In their quarterfinal on Thursday versus Canada West rival British Columbia at the U Sports Women’s Final 8 in Quebec City, the fifth- ranked Huskies shot just 24 per cent from three-point range and 48 per cent from the free throw line, but overcame those struggles by locking down the fourth- ranked Thunderbirds. Holding UBC to 34 per cent shooting from the field and never allowing more than 15 points in any quarter, the Huskies advanced to Saturday’s semifinal with a 71-55 victory.

Winners of U Sports crowns in 2016, 2020 and 2025, the Huskies have another national title within reach. The team, undefeated in the regular season, lost in the Canada West semifinal but received the lone wild card to nationals.

“I think if we can defend like that and we can have a bit better shooting night, then we’ll give ourselves a chance no matter who we’re playing against,” Huskies head coach Lisa Thomaidis said following the game.

First-team all-Canadian Gage Grassick led the Huskies with a game-high 25 points, while adding three steals and four rebounds. Ella Murphy Wiebe scored 16 points and hauled down eight rebounds. Maya Flindell and Andrea Dodig each scored 11 points for the Huskies. Sara Toneguzzi had a team-high 21 points for UBC.

In Saturday’s semifinal, the Huskies take on No. 8- and host Laval. In a first-round upset, the Rouge et Or knocked off top-ranked Toronto Metropolitan 57-54. Sabrine Khelifi, who led her team with 14 points, banked a three-pointer from the top of the arc with 15 seconds left in the fourth quarter to break the tie and propel her squad to the medal round.

The Huskies tip off Saturday against Laval at 4 p.m. On the other side of the bracket, Canada West champion and third-ranked Calgary Dinos advanced with a 58-45 win over sixth-ranked McGill. In Saturday’s other semifinal, Calgary faces off against No. 2-ranked New Brunswick, which handled seventh-ranked Carleton Ravens 80-63.

Title: Flight paths squeezed as Iran conflict closes more airspace
Author: Unknown
Summary: The closure of southern Azerbaijani airspace due to regional conflicts is causing significant disruption for airlines, forcing longer reroutes north or south and increasing flight times and fuel costs. Gulf airlines are increasing flights from the UAE and Oman to evacuate travelers, but overall capacity remains low. Air traffic has sharply decreased, complicating airline planning amid heightened tensions.
Content:
The closure of airspace over the southern half of Azerbaijan is putting additional pressure on airlines responding to disruptions caused by the conflict in the Gulf. Flight traffic had already been severely disrupted in a region that normally acts as a significant hub for the global airline industry. The Buta drone attack on a small Central Asian country has squeezed many flights into a narrow corridor across the north of Azerbaijan.

Aviation expert John Strickland noted there is now a "very tight range of options for airlines" trying to navigate the current situation. Western airlines have had to avoid Russian airspace, including Siberia, since the invasion of Ukraine in 2022. Now, all flights are also steering clear of Iran and Iraq airspace, opting for alternative routes to the north or south.

Flightradar24 data maps show real-time congestion in an approximately 100 km wide strip across the north of Azerbaijan, a country roughly the size of Portugal. Late on Friday, data indicated planes were using northern Azerbaijan's airspace but not flying over the south of the country.

Gulf airlines have begun operating more flights out of the UAE and Oman to evacuate stranded travelers, but there are no flights out of Qatar, and capacity remains well below normal levels, according to Opsgroup, which provides risk information for the industry. "The central corridor across Iran, Iraq and the Gulf is effectively closed, so most traffic is going either north via the Caucasus and Afghanistan, or south via Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Oman," said David Mumford, an international operations specialist at Opsgroup. "Both routes are longer and busier than usual, so flight times and fuel burn are higher."

Air traffic sharply decreased after the attacks began on Saturday as airlines were forced to use more limited corridors. Aviation consultant Strickland said passengers could experience longer flight times due to extended routing. "Flights already doing circuitous routing have even less choice," he added.

Some airlines have been adjusting routes in recent weeks as tensions in the Gulf increased. This week, even more flights were forced to switch to routes further south to avoid the conflict zone. Congestion is expected during busy times, such as west to east in the afternoon and evening when Europe-to-Asia flights are scheduled, and east to west early in the morning when flights from Asia to Europe arrive. This presents "another big complication for airlines' planning," on top of usual factors like weather and the impact of delays on subsequent flights.

Most airlines are not currently adding stops to accommodate longer routes, but Qantas has added a refueling stop in Singapore on its non-stop flight from Perth to London.

Title: OSHA probing fatality at Rivian warehouse
Author: Sean O'Kane
Summary: OSHA is investigating the death of 61-year-old Kevin Lancaster at a Rivian warehouse in Illinois, where he was pinned between a tractor trailer and a loading dock. Lancaster died from blunt traumatic injuries after being trapped for 20 minutes. Rivian expressed condolences and is cooperating with authorities. The factory has faced safety scrutiny, but violations have decreased since 2024.
Content:
The Occupational Safety and Health Administration has opened an investigation into the death of a worker at a Rivian warehouse in Illinois this week, the federal agency told Tech Crunch on Friday. The agency said the probe could take up to six months.

The man, identified by local authorities as 61-year-old Kevin Lancaster, reportedly died from “blunt traumatic compressional injuries” after getting pinned between a tractor trailer and a loading dock at the facility, which is located just a few miles from Rivian’s factory. Lancaster was reportedly trapped in that spot for around 20 minutes on Thursday before firefighters were able to reach him, according to a local news report. Emergency crews responded to a call at 1:40 p.m. local time, and Lancaster was pronounced dead at a local medical center at 2:33 p.m. local time. The Normal Police Department and the McLean County Coroner are still investigating Lancaster’s death, according to the report.

“Safety at our facilities is our top priority. Unfortunately yesterday afternoon, a contractor passed away after an incident at our warehouse,” Rivian said in a statement to Tech Crunch. “Our sympathy and thoughts are with their family and friends. We are working with the Normal Police Department on its investigation.”

The safety of Rivian’s factory in Normal, Illinois, became a source of scrutiny in 2024 after a Bloomberg News report detailed 16 “serious” violations levied on the company across that year and the one prior. Automotive factories are notoriously dangerous, though, and Rivian has received just one violation at the Illinois manufacturing plant since that report was published. OSHA even told Bloomberg at the time that Rivian “has improved their safety and health team and are very cooperative with the OSHA process.”

Rivian assembles its flagship R1 pickup truck, R1 SUV, and commercial electric van, known as the EDV, at its 4.3-million-square-foot factory in Normal. The company is expanding the space by another 1.1 million square feet to make room for its next EV, the R2. Once complete, the factory will have capacity to assemble 215,000 vehicles.

Title: King, Domani Strike Back at 50 Cent with Two Angry Diss Tracks
Author: Trent Fitzgerald
Summary: King and Domani released diss tracks on Instagram, retaliating against 50 Cent for dissing T.I. and the Harris family on the Power Origins theme song. King’s "Droptop" criticizes 50 Cent, while Domani’s "PU$Y" questions his toughness. 50 Cent responded with provocative posts, escalating the feud.
Content:
King and Domani dropped two diss tracks after 50 Cent appeared to have dissed T.I. and the Harris family on the new Power Origins theme song. On Friday (March 6), King and Domani released the songs on their Instagram pages in retaliation against 50 Cent for disrespecting T.I. and the Harris family.

King's diss track is called "Droptop." In the song, he criticizes 50 Cent for talking too much and accuses him of allegedly beating women. "You were made from 50 Cent because ya mama gave it cheap," King raps.

Meanwhile, Domani's diss track titled "PU$Y" targets 50 Cent with a haunting tone. He questions 50's toughness, rapping, "I smell p***y/How about you come here and try to push me/That suga water got ya, now you turn sweet, huh/I smell p***y/Ni**a, I read you like a book read/Come bring ya a*s to school quit playin' hookie/I smell p***y."

These songs respond to 50 Cent, who featured T.I. and the Harris family on the theme song for his upcoming television show, Power Origins. The track, featuring R&B singer Leon Thomas, includes street rap about the drug trade. 50 Cent raps, "I'm back on my dope boy grammar/Your daddy made your mama eat every box in Atlanta," and mentions secret activities with lines like, "Freak sh*t/Peep sh*t/Keep sh*t, on the low/But everybody know." 50 captioned his Instagram post about the song, "50cent Ft Leon Thomas (No one told us what we’re here for) POWER ORIGINS theme song!"

Further escalating the feud, 50 Cent posted a photoshopped image of T.I. with Tiny eating from a cut-up cardboard box, possibly implying that 50's lyric was directed at T.I. and the Harris family. He captioned the post, "LOL...They are gonna learn to leave me alone."

The feud between 50 Cent and the Harris family is intensifying.

Title: Grading the Maple Leafs & Kings’ Scott Laughton Trade
Author: Spencer Lazary
Summary: The Toronto Maple Leafs traded Scott Laughton to the Los Angeles Kings for a conditional 2026 third-round pick, as part of multiple deadline deals aimed at recouping draft capital and managing cap space. The Leafs also traded Nicolas Roy and Bobby McMann for draft picks. The moves reflect a strategic sell-off rather than immediate roster improvement. The Kings seek playoff boost with Laughton’s addition.
Content:
The Toronto Maple Leafs left everything to the final buzzer. All day, Leafs Nation was wondering what deals the organization would make to help this team retool. Yesterday, they traded Nicolas Roy to the Colorado Avalanche; today, they dealt Bobby McMann to the Seattle Kraken. Now, they have announced that they have traded Scott Laughton to the Los Angeles Kings.

Laughton was acquired just one year ago at the 2025 NHL Trade Deadline. Now, he is on his way out of town and heading to California to try and help the Kings push for a playoff spot down the stretch. As for the Maple Leafs, their selling of assets continues.

Full Trade:
Maple Leafs receive: Conditional 2026 third-round pick  
Kings receive: Scott Laughton  
Maple Leafs Restocking Draft Capital  
Trade Grade: C

The Maple Leafs have made three trades over the last 24 hours, all of which have resulted in draft picks. This approach is ideal for Toronto, who are trying to maximize cap space heading into next season and recoup draft capital for players on expiring contracts. No fan within Leafs Nation should have expected them to pull off a hockey trade. It was always going to be a cap dump for draft picks, or a major trade for someone like Robert Thomas. It makes more sense for them to sell assets for picks and then look at how to retool this roster in the offseason, rather than trying to fix it at the deadline.

Brad Treliving did have the right approach in selling their UFAs. Unfortunately, he had to budge on his asking price. He was able to get a first and a fifth for Roy, a second and a fourth for McMann. However, he was only able to secure a conditional third-round pick, which slides to a second if the Kings make the playoffs. While that is a decent return, it is significantly less than what he paid for Laughton just a season ago, which was Nikita Grebennik and a conditional 2027 first-round pick. It would have been a better return if he could have acquired a player like Alex Turcotte or a mid-tier prospect along with the conditional pick. Regardless, he was able to sell an expiring asset, which should set them up well in the draft. The only hope now is that the Kings can play well enough to make the playoffs.

From the Kings’ perspective, the cost for Laughton is minimal. They are giving up a reliable depth center as they try to improve their playoff chances. The Kings sit three points out of the final wild card spot and have made some significant organizational changes over the past month, including acquiring Artemi Panarin before the Winter Olympic roster freeze, firing head coach Jim Hiller, and hiring D.J. Smith. They also dealt Warren Foegele and Corey Perry, adding Laughton as a third or fourth center depending on their lineup needs.

P&G faces margin pressures from rising costs but maintains steady sales through brand strength and innovation. American Express has declined 5.1% over six months amid rising expenses and credit-loss provisions. The reports also cover micro-cap stocks Genie Energy and Comp X International.
