Title: Armed robots take to the battlefield in Ukraine war
Author: Unknown
Summary: Since Russia's invasion of Ukraine, armed ground robots (UGVs) are increasingly used in combat, with Ukraine deploying them to repel attacks and capture soldiers. These semi-autonomous robots, equipped with weapons and kamikaze units, operate under human control to prevent misfires. Most are remotely controlled, with logistical and defensive roles also prominent. Experts predict rapid expansion of UGVs in future warfare.
Content:
Armed robots are increasingly being used on the battlefield in Ukraine. Since Russia's full-scale invasion, the conflict has become highly technological, with swarms of drones in the skies and uncrewed boats disrupting the Russian navy in the Black Sea. Ukraine has launched a large program to deploy armed ground robots, known as uncrewed ground vehicles (UGVs), which have already demonstrated their effectiveness. There have been reports of UGVs repelling Russian attacks and capturing enemy soldiers. Ukrainian and Russian killer robots are even said to have engaged without human presence at the scene.

Oleksandr Afanasiev from the Ukrainian army's K2 brigade, which he claims is the world's first UGV battalion, states that "robot wars are already happening." The brigade has been mounting Kalashnikov machine guns on these robots, allowing them to operate in dangerous battlefield areas where infantry would be at risk. Afanasiev explains that these robots are willing to risk their existence to open fire in situations too perilous for human soldiers.

Ukrainian UGVs are also equipped with explosive-laden, battery-powered kamikaze units designed to destroy enemy positions silently, without alerting opponents to their approach. The deputy commander of the 33rd Detached Mechanised Brigade's tank battalion, known by the callsign Afghan, reports that a Ukrainian UGV with a machine gun ambushed a Russian personnel carrier and that a robot has defended a Ukrainian position for weeks.

Afghan emphasizes that many of these killer robots operate with limited autonomy due to ethical and legal considerations. "Modern UGVs are part-autonomous. They can move, observe, and detect the enemy, but the decision to open fire is made by a human operator," he states. This is to prevent misidentification and unintended harm to civilians, ensuring that final decisions remain under human control.

Most armed UGVs are remotely controlled over the internet from safe distances. Ukraine's lethal robots can be equipped with grenade launchers, machine guns, and can be used to plant landmines or barbed wire. However, the majority of UGVs are still employed for logistical tasks such as delivering supplies and evacuating the wounded.

Valerii Zaluzhnyi, Ukraine's former commander-in-chief and now ambassador to the UK, predicts that the use of armed UGVs will expand rapidly. Speaking at Chatham House, he discussed how strike UGVs are expected to play an increasingly prominent role in future warfare.

Title: One Suspect Charged for Foolio's Murder Wants Separate Trial
Author: Trent Fitzgerald
Summary: Rashad Murphy, charged in Foolio's 2024 murder, requests a separate trial or jury due to potential evidence overlap with co-defendants. His attorneys filed a supplemental memorandum addressing this issue. Murphy is one of five suspects; Alicia Andrews was convicted of manslaughter, with sentencing scheduled for December. The other suspects' motions were denied in court.
Content:
One of the suspects charged with Foolio's murder is to have his own trial. According to documents obtained by XXL, attorneys for Rashad Murphy, one of the suspects in the 2024 murder of rapper Foolio, filed a supplemental memorandum on Thursday (March 5) requesting to be tried separately from his co-defendants for their upcoming trial, or at least have a different jury. Last month, the court held a hearing to address the motion but no ruling was made.

On Friday (March 6), Murphy's attorneys filed an amended memorandum addressing the court's indication that evidence concerning a separate shooting involving Murphy's co-defendants might be admissible against all defendants. This would happen if the judge determines that the evidence is "inextricably intertwined" with the charges related to Foolio's killing. 

Florida law requires a defendant-specific relevance and prejudice analysis in joint trials, Rashad Murphy respectfully submits this supplemental memorandum to address that issue, the motion reads.

Rashad Murphy is one of five people charged with Foolio's murder, along with Alicia Andrews, Isaiah Chance, Sean Gathright, and Davion Murphy. The Jacksonville, Fla., rapper was shot and killed by three masked gunmen while celebrating his birthday in Tampa, Fla., on June 23, 2004.

In October 2025, Andrews was convicted of manslaughter in connection with Foolio's killing and faces a maximum sentence of 15 years in prison. Her sentencing was delayed after the original judge was removed in January by an appellate court due to bias. Andrews’ new sentencing hearing is now scheduled for December 8.

Meanwhile, the four other suspects—Rashad, Davion, Isaiah, and Sean Gathright—were in court on Wednesday (March 4), where a judge denied several motions, including some Florida statutes, the death penalty, and victim impact evidence.

Title: Assessing the Blackhawks Trade Deadline Moves, & What It Means for the Future
Author: Gail Kauchak
Summary: The Chicago Blackhawks traded defenseman Connor Murphy to the Edmonton Oilers for a 2028 second-round pick, retaining half his $4.4 million cap hit. This move opens opportunities for younger defensemen like Rinzel and Levshunov. The team, in a rebuilding phase, faces increased responsibility for remaining players, testing their readiness for future roles.
Content:
It’s been a very, very busy week for the Chicago Blackhawks. The trade deadline has come and gone, and the rebuilding Blackhawks were sellers once again. General manager Kyle Davidson can be commended for gaining some value out of a few expiring veteran contracts, as well as doing right by the players.

In the meantime, a much younger and still very inexperienced team is being tasked with taking the next step in their progression, even though a sizeable portion of their support system has been stripped away. Different players will need to step up and fill new roles both on and off the ice. But they’ve been schooled for this. Are they ready for it? I guess we’ll find out.

Let’s discuss the ramifications of this year’s trade deadline moves for the Blackhawks.

Murphy Moved to Edmonton

The first move was on Monday, Mar. 2, when defenseman Connor Murphy was traded to the Edmonton Oilers for a 2028 second-round draft pick. Chicago also retained half of Murphy’s $4.4 million cap hit on his contract, which expires at the end of this season. While it’s tough to see the longest-tenured member of the Blackhawks go (nine seasons), he wasn’t going to be re-signed next season. Younger defensemen are ready to take his place on the blue line.

Murphy will have a chance to finally play postseason hockey, which he’s never done in his 13 NHL seasons (except for the COVID bubble playoff in 2020). He’ll be 33 years old on Mar. 26, so time is ticking for the stay-at-home defenseman. Now he’ll have a chance to fulfill that dream.

This move opens up opportunities for other right-handed defensemen on the team to take on bigger roles. 21-year-old Sam Rinzel and 20-year-old Artyom Levshunov will both be given the reins to become everyday players. With Murphy there, head coach Jeff Blashill had to deploy seven defensemen to get them both in the lineup. Rinzel spent some time with the Rockford Ice Hogs, and Levshunov was a healthy scratch for three contests before the Olympic break for a “reset” to help solidify better defensive play. Hopefully, both situations have prepared the young blueliners to take on additional responsibilities and ice time.

Alex Vlasic and Louis Crevier have become the top shutdown pairing for the Blackhawks, but now they’re really going to be tested. With Murphy, the team started in the defensive zone 75.8% of the time. Someone will need to replace that. In the contest against the Winnipeg Jets on Mar. 3—the Hawks’ first game without Murphy—Vlasic started 18 defensive shifts.

Title: Trump demands 'unconditional surrender' from Iran as Putin speaks with Iran's president
Author: Unknown
Summary: US President Donald Trump demanded Iran's "unconditional surrender" amid ongoing US and Israeli strikes, with no interest in negotiated settlement. He emphasized strengthening Iran's economy post-conflict and expressed a desire to influence Iran's leadership, rejecting radical or military-led successors. The White House expects the military operations to last four to six weeks.
Content:
US President Donald Trump has demanded Iran's "unconditional surrender" as the American and Israeli military continued to launch strikes. Trump said the US and allies would make Iran "economically bigger, better, and stronger than ever before" after it picked "acceptable" new leadership.

With the war close to entering a second week, it remains unclear who will lead Iran after the killing of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei a week ago, or on what terms the US would end its offensive against Tehran, which has triggered region-wide retaliatory strikes. Speaking on Friday, White House spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said the administration expected military operations to last four to six weeks.

Writing on his Truth Social platform, Trump said: "There will be no deal with Iran except UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER! After that, and the selection of a GREAT & ACCEPTABLE Leader(s), we, and many of our wonderful and very brave allies and partners, will work tirelessly to bring Iran back from the brink of destruction, making it economically bigger, better, and stronger than ever before. IRAN WILL HAVE A GREAT FUTURE. MAKE IRAN GREAT AGAIN (MIGA!)."

Trump's latest intervention suggested he was not interested in a negotiated settlement to the war, though he stopped short of calling for regime change. Since the beginning of the conflict, Trump has avoided the term "regime change," a sign he may be wary of drawing parallels between the attack on Iran and past US wars in the Middle East that he criticised before entering politics.

Other senior administration officials have made that argument on Trump's behalf in recent days, saying the president will not let the US get drawn into a protracted, open-ended conflict like in Iraq and Afghanistan. Asked about the duration of the offensive, Leavitt told reporters: "We expect [the war] to last about 4 to 6 weeks, and we are well on our way to achieving those objectives, annihilating Iran's navy."

She also said Trump did not want Iran to be led by "a radical terrorist or a regime that chants death to America." The White House press secretary told reporters: "[Trump] wants to take an interest in and pursue who the next leader of the Iranian country is going to be. And that has yet to be decided."

Earlier this week, Trump told US outlet Axios he expected to play a role in the selection of Iran's next leader. Responding to reports that Khamenei's son Mojtaba — a conservative close to Iran's powerful Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps military wing — could be installed as leader, Trump said that would be "unacceptable."

Title: Robinhood’s startup fund stumbles in NYSE debut
Author: Marina Temkin
Summary: Robinhood launched Robinhood Ventures Fund I to allow retail investors to buy into private startups, including Databricks, Stripe, and others. The fund raised $658.4 million out of a $705.7 million target, with shares trading below initial price. Unlike Destiny Tech100, which holds high-profile VC-backed companies and trades at a premium, Robinhood’s fund lacks exposure to companies expected to go public at high valuations.
Content:
Retail investors are often excluded from the startup world. Robinhood is attempting to change that by allowing the public to invest in a portfolio of private companies it considers "some of the most exciting private companies operating today." To do this, Robinhood has secured access to eight startups—including Databricks, Stripe, Mercor, and Oura—grouped into a vehicle called Robinhood Ventures Fund I. The fund also includes Ramp, Airwallex, Revolut, and Boom. It launched last month with a target of $1 billion, but demand was lower than expected.

On Thursday, Robinhood announced the fund had raised $658.4 million, which could reach $705.7 million if underwriters exercise their full allotment. The shares, priced at $25, began trading on Friday and closed at $21, a 16% decline.

The reception of Robinhood Ventures (RVI) contrasts sharply with another effort to give retail investors exposure to high-profile startups. When Destiny Tech100, a publicly traded fund holding stakes in 100 venture-backed companies including SpaceX, OpenAI, and Discord, listed on the NYSE in March 2024, its shares surged from a reference price of $4.84 to an opening trade of $8.25, eventually closing at $9.00. Since then, Destiny Tech100 has continued to rise, closing Friday at $26.61, a 33% premium to its net asset value of $19.97, indicating its shares trade well above the value of its underlying holdings.

The key difference appears to be that Robinhood’s fund lacks exposure to companies expected to go public at high valuations, such as OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX. Robinhood plans to address this by adding more startups to the fund, aiming to hold "15 to 20 of the best late-stage growth companies," according to Robinhood Ventures President Sarah Pinto. The company’s CFO, Shiv Verma, mentioned that Robinhood is also considering exposure to OpenAI.

Title: Marathon interactive maps
Author: Polygon Staff
Summary: The article details interactive maps for the game Marathon, featuring two launch zones—Perimeter and Dire Marsh—and an upcoming Cryo Archive map. Perimeter is beginner-friendly but challenging, with hotspots like Hauler and Tunnels. Dire Marsh is swampy with stronger enemies, while Outpost is a tough, close-quarters UESC facility. Maps help locate locked rooms, exfil points, and key targets.
Content:
No Ads Ninja v10 Client!

Jump Links Perimeter interactive map Dire Marsh interactive map Outpost interactive map Whether you're looking for locked rooms, exfil locations, or priority targets, our Marathon interactive maps are what you need. There are two zones in Marathon at launch — Perimeter and Dire Marsh — and each of them offers a different environment to hunt down other runners and UESC enemies alike, before getting out with your loot. Here are our interactive Marathon maps for Perimeter, Dire Marsh, and Outpost. Expect an interactive map for the game's hardest map, Cryo Archive, when it becomes playable later this month.

Perimeter interactive map Considered the best map for beginners, don't let that fool you. Perimeter can still give you a tough time. Hauler is one of the most active player hotspots here, as one of the only ways from the southern half of the map to the north, due to the Data Wall that stretches horizontally. Then there's Tunnels, a mazy network of underground tunnels that houses plenty of high-tier loot. Not to mention Station and Overflow, which you must complete the "Survival Directive" priority contract.

Dire Marsh interactive map Dire Marsh is a swampy environment, with Algae Ponds being the most populated; some players have referred to it as "Marathon's Tilted Towers." You can complete "Introducing: Traxus" at Intersection, Bio-Research, or Complex, and there's also the Maintenance Pump key you can use in the southwest corner. The UESC threats on Dire Marsh are stronger than on Perimeter, so make sure you're using the best weapons and classes.

Outpost interactive map Outpost is a close-quarters map that takes place within a massive UESC facility. The enemies are tougher, extraction is more difficult, and even the air itself will sometimes combust into hellfire. This map is brutal for new runners. Bring good equipment before you tackle this map.

Title: Surfers in Munich (Yes, Munich) Just Want Their Wave Back
Author: Christopher F. Schuetze and Laetitia Vançon
Summary: Two men in Munich attempted to recreate the city's famous artificial wave at the Eisbach creek, which was removed after dredging last year. Authorities warn of safety concerns following a recent death and require a fixed wave maker. The surfers' efforts to install a new obstacle have been unsuccessful due to the creek’s strong current dislodging the equipment.
Content:
Ignoring the winter freeze from the Alps, two men shed their parkas one recent morning, squirmed into wet suits, and walked into a raging creek in downtown Munich. A helper handed them a 20-foot metal bar, which the men shoved underwater. They were trying to create a wave by installing an obstacle that would bend the churning water to surfable perfection. And they were failing.

Munich, despite sitting some 200 miles from the nearest sea, was an unlikely surf spot until recently. Enthusiasts flocked to the waterway, called the Eisbach, in a park near a university neighborhood, to take turns riding the artificial wave. But after the authorities dredged the channel last year, the wave vanished.

Now the Munich surfing community — yes, there is such a thing — is trying to remake it. Nature and bureaucracy have complicated things. The city authorities have urged caution after a death at the creek last year. They want the surfers to install a safer kind of wave maker — one that is fixed to the creek bed, unlike the old version, which hung from a bridge.

The problem is that the surfers’ efforts to satisfy that demand have not yet made waves: when the men in wet suits jammed the metal bar into the creek bed, the Eisbach’s current kept dislodging it.

Title: For Xi, Trump’s Embrace of War Proves China Needs More Power
Author: David Pierson
Summary: U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran, including killing Iran’s supreme leader, highlight China's view that military power is essential. Xi Jinping has long prioritized building a strong military to deter U.S. aggression, viewing American use of force as a threat to China's rise. Despite differences, China sees U.S. military actions as reaffirming its need for a formidable defense.
Content:
The sudden and furious attacks by U.S. and Israeli forces on Iran this past week, including the killing of the country’s supreme leader, are confirming Xi Jinping’s worldview that hard power is king.

For years, Mr. Xi, China’s top leader, has warned his country about American military hostility and directed his generals to build a world-class army, or what he called a “Great Wall of Steel,” strong enough to deter the United States and ensure peace on Beijing’s terms. “It is necessary to speak to invaders in the language they know,” Mr. Xi said. “That is, a war must be fought to deter invasion, and a victory is needed to win peace and respect.”

Mr. Xi’s more than decade-long pursuit of the power to fight fire with fire is now given added urgency because of President Trump’s unapologetic use of military force, which is threatening governments and disrupting a global order that China increasingly sees itself as eventually leading. While Mr. Xi and Mr. Trump are set to meet in several weeks in Beijing to extend a fragile truce after a punishing trade war last year, the specter of brute American force taking down sovereign leaders like Iran’s Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and capturing others like President Nicolás Maduro of Venezuela remains unsettling for China. Both Ayatollah Khamenei and Mr. Maduro were strategic partners to Beijing.

Chinese analysts and officials do not believe the United States would target Mr. Xi in the same way, given China’s status as a nuclear power. But Mr. Trump’s embrace of war as a tool to assert American dominance has nonetheless reaffirmed Beijing’s view that the United States remains China’s most enduring threat.

Title: We've been speaking to Iranians during one week of war. Here's what they said
Author: Unknown
Summary: During the first week of war, Iranians expressed mixed emotions—some cheered regime targets and felt hope for change after Khamenei's death, while others felt fear, uncertainty, and skepticism about the war's motives. Despite internet restrictions and repression, BBC Persian gathered insights from inside and outside Iran, revealing a population divided between hope for regime change and concern over escalating violence.
Content:
We've been speaking to Iranians during one week of war. Here's what they said.

On Monday, three days into the war, men in Tehran watched from a hillside as a plume of smoke rose after an explosion.

When Hamid heard news of the death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei a week ago, he felt a wave of euphoria and took his wife and daughter into the street outside his home in Tehran to celebrate. For the next few days, as US and Israeli bombs slammed into buildings across the capital, the family went onto the roof of the house to watch the airstrikes coming in, cheering every time a regime target was hit.

"Try to find anywhere else on this earth where the population would be happy with an external attack on their country," he told me, via a cousin in the UK. "But we now have hope that the regime will soon be gone. We are happy."

Hamid—not his real name—is not alone. Alongside colleagues from BBC Persian, we have been hearing from people inside and outside Iran on a momentous week for them, for the future of their country, and for the entire region. BBC Persian is the Persian language service of BBC News, used by 24 million people around the world—majority in Iran—despite being blocked and routinely jammed by Iranian authorities.

In a police state under bombardment, with severe internet restrictions in place, it is impossible to fully gauge the mood across a vast nation of 90 million people. Residents of Tehran have received messages warning: "If your connection to the internet continues in the coming days, your line will be blocked and you will be referred to judicial authorities."

The regime still instills fear, and no one who voices opposition is willing to give their name for fear of repercussions on themselves or their families. But a week on, while some are still celebrating every strike on the regime, others are increasingly frightened, questioning the motives and endgame of the war.

"The goal of this war isn't to bring about freedom or democracy for the Iranian people," Ali told us. "It's for the geopolitical benefit of Israel, the US, and Arab countries in the region."

Mohammad, in his 30s and living in Tehran, said he had wanted a deal between the US and Iran that would have avoided war. "Deep down, I always hoped an agreement would be made," he said. He thought he would feel relief at Khamenei's death, but in the end "felt nothing." He told my colleague Soroush Pakzad that he is now filled with uncertainty about the future, and, with regime checkpoints everywhere and bombardments from above, he is afraid.

Other Iranians speak of feeling a mix of fear, stress, and hope. One woman told me that I would have to live in Iran for 40 years to understand the complexity of what she and others are now feeling. "We laugh and are happy when the regime is..."

Title: Live Updates: New Wave of Airstrikes Batters Tehran
Author: The New York Times
Summary: The Israeli military launched extensive strikes on Iranian infrastructure, including near Mehrabad Airport in Tehran, causing damage and fires. Iran’s IRGC responded with drone and missile attacks on Israel. The conflict has expanded to Lebanon with strikes on Hezbollah targets, prompting evacuations. U.S. officials suspect Russia is providing Iran with intelligence, complicating regional tensions amid rising global oil prices.
Content:
The Israeli military said it had launched “a broad-scale wave of strikes” on Iranian government infrastructure in the early hours of Saturday morning. Soon after, Iranian state media reported that Israeli strikes had hit the area near Mehrabad Airport in Tehran. Two residents living near the airport indicated that it appeared to have been badly damaged, with some suggesting that commercial planes were burning on the tarmac. The Israeli military did not immediately respond to questions about whether it had targeted the airport.

These strikes in Tehran followed reports that U.S. officials believe Russia has provided intelligence to Iran during the conflict, including satellite imagery showing the locations of warships and military personnel. This involvement of Russia marks a further expansion of the Middle East conflict. While some officials downplayed its significance, noting that Russia has long shared similar intelligence with Iran, it could complicate relations between Washington and Moscow.

Bombing campaigns continued in Iran and Lebanon on Friday, contributing to a surge in oil and gas prices, reflecting the global economic impact of the nearly week-long U.S.-Israeli war against Iran. President Trump demanded “unconditional surrender” by Iran via social media, marking the most uncompromising stance so far and suggesting a potential prolongation of the conflict.

Israeli officials announced that they had destroyed an underground bunker used by Iran’s supreme leader before his death last week. The Israeli military also targeted the southern outskirts of Beirut and issued evacuation warnings in Lebanon amid intensified operations against Iran-backed Hezbollah militants. An estimated 300,000 people in Lebanon have fled their homes since the beginning of the bombing, according to the Norwegian Refugee Council.

Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps launched a wave of drones and missiles at Israel, according to a statement from the force reported by IRNA, Iran’s state news agency. Air-raid sirens sounded in Tel Aviv, and the Israeli military reported detecting missile launches from Iran, though no major damage was immediately reported.

The Israeli military said it struck more than 400 targets in western Iran on Friday, including missile launchers and drone storage sites.

Title: Conflict spreads across the Middle East in days after U.S.-Israel attack kills Iran's supreme leader
Author: Sarah Petz
Summary: Conflict in the Middle East escalates after the U.S.-Israel attack kills Iran's supreme leader. Iran retaliates with strikes on Israel and U.S. bases, while Israel targets Hezbollah in Lebanon. Hundreds have died, thousands evacuated, and a humanitarian crisis unfolds. U.S. calls for "unconditional surrender" from Iran; Canada evacuates 3,500 citizens. Death tolls exceed 1,230 in Iran and 217 in Lebanon.
Content:
Conflict spreads across the Middle East days after the U.S.-Israel attack kills Iran's supreme leader. Countries are rushing to evacuate citizens as Iran retaliates for ongoing strikes. Rescuers inspect the site of an Israel and U.S. strike on a police station in Tehran, Iran, on March 3.

The U.S.-Israeli war on Iran continues to widen after nearly a week, leaving hundreds dead, thousands seeking evacuation, and many facing a humanitarian crisis. Iran launched retaliatory attacks on Israel and U.S. bases, while Israel intensified its campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah militants in Lebanon.

On Friday, U.S. President Donald Trump stated that the only deal with Iran to potentially end the war would be "unconditional surrender." Prime Minister Mark Carney said Canada had no plans to engage militarily as of Friday, focusing instead on evacuating 3,500 Canadians who requested help from the region.

The death tolls as of Friday include more than 1,230 people in Iran. The Lebanese Health Ministry reported 217 deaths and more than 790 wounded due to Israeli attacks.

Title: Fresh Energy In March (2026 Wallpapers Edition)
Author: hello@smashingmagazine.com (Cosima Mielke)
Summary: Smashing Magazine has released a new collection of free desktop wallpapers, designed by global artists to celebrate spring and inspire creativity. The collection features diverse designs, including "Timid Blossom" by Ginger from Serbia. This monthly tradition has lasted over 14 years, encouraging artists to submit their work for future editions.
Content:
If you need a little inspiration boost, our new collection of desktop wallpapers is for you. Designed by the community for the community, the wallpapers in this collection are the perfect opportunity to get your desktop ready for spring — and, who knows, maybe they’ll spark some new ideas, too.

Blooming flowers, longer days, milder temperatures — with March just around the corner, the world is slowly but surely awakening from its winter slumber, fueling us with fresh energy. And even if spring is far away in your part of the world, you might sense that 2026 has gained full speed by now, making it the perfect moment to turn those plans and ideas you’ve been carrying around into action.

To accompany you on all those adventures that March may bring, we have a new collection of desktop wallpapers for you, just as it has been a monthly tradition here at Smashing Magazine for more than 14 years already. Designed by artists and designers from across the globe, each wallpaper comes in a variety of screen resolutions and can be downloaded for free. A huge thank-you to everyone who shared their designs with us — this post wouldn’t be possible without your kind support!

If you, too, would like to get featured in one of our upcoming wallpapers editions, please don’t hesitate to submit your design. We look forward to seeing what you come up with!

Happy March! You can click on every image to see a larger preview.

We respect and carefully consider the ideas and motivation behind each artist’s work. This is why we give all artists the full freedom to explore their creativity and express emotions and experiences through their works. The themes of the wallpapers weren’t influenced by us but were designed from scratch by the artists themselves.

One of the featured designs is titled "Timid Blossom," created by Ginger from Serbia, which depicts blossoms heralding spring amidst seasonal transitions.

Title: NFL free agency grades: Who won the Ravens-Raiders trade involving Maxx Crosby?
Author: Seth Walder
Summary: The Bears agreed to trade wide receiver DJ Moore to the Bills ahead of NFL free agency. Other notable moves include the Rams trading four draft picks for cornerback Trent McDuffie and the Ravens acquiring edge rusher Maxx Crosby from the Raiders for two first-round picks. Signings, such as the Chargers signing Tyler Biadasz, are also underway.
Content:
Adam Schefter reports that the Bears have agreed to trade DJ Moore to the Buffalo Bills.

Multiple trades and signings have already taken place ahead of NFL free agency, which begins March 9 with the legal negotiating window. The Rams sent four draft picks to the Chiefs for star cornerback Trent McDuffie, and the Bills acquired a standout wide receiver in DJ Moore in a deal with the Bears. The biggest trade so far involved the Ravens sending two first-round picks to the Raiders for edge rusher Maxx Crosby. Signings have also started, with the Chargers signing former Commanders center Tyler Biadasz to a three-year deal.

ESPN analytics writer Seth Walder is grading the biggest signings and trades of the offseason, evaluating each move based on factors such as on-field impact, salary cap implications, player value and age, and the teams' short- and long-term outlooks. His assessments consider how each decision affects a team's chances of winning the Super Bowl this season or in the future.

One notable trade is the Ravens acquiring Maxx Crosby from the Raiders in exchange for two first-round picks. Walder expressed surprise that the Ravens were the team to trade for Crosby and noted the high cost of the deal. Crosby is considered an exceptional player, with three double-digit sack seasons in the past four years, five Pro Bowl selections, and participation in 110 of 117 possible regular-season games over his seven-year career. His pass rush win rate is 17.2%, placing him in the 79th percentile, and he has accumulated 222 pass-rush wins over that span, the fourth-most in the league. Crosby is also highly effective against the run, contributing to his ability to stay on the field for nearly all defensive snaps.

Title: Maxx Crosby to the Ravens: What's next for both teams, plus free agency and draft implications
Author: Jamison Hensley, Ryan McFadden, Dan Graziano, Matt Miller
Summary: The Raiders traded defensive end Maxx Crosby to the Ravens for 2026 and 2027 first-round picks. Crosby, a five-time Pro Bowler with 69.5 career sacks, impressed Baltimore during a September 2024 game. The Ravens aim to improve their pass rush, which was a noted weakness last season.
Content:
The Raiders agreed to trade defensive end Maxx Crosby to the Ravens for 2026 and 2027 first-round picks, according to sources. Crosby, 28, is a five-time Pro Bowler and has recorded 69.5 career sacks since 2019, more than double the total of any Ravens player over that span. His performance in a September 2024 game against Baltimore, where he recorded six tackles, four tackles for loss, and two sacks—including one that sacked quarterback Lamar Jackson—impressed the Ravens enough to pursue him. 

The Ravens sought to bolster their pass rush, which they identified as a weakness this past season. General manager Eric De Costa emphasized the importance of upgrading at edge rusher during the NFL combine, noting that a ferocious pass rush is key to strong defenses.

Title: Kit: An Opinionated API Starter Kit for Laravel
Author: Yannick Lyn Fatt
Summary: Kit by Steve Mc Dougallis is a Laravel API starter kit featuring token-based authentication, JSON:API responses, security defaults, and sunset middleware. It supports versioned routes, invokable controllers, form request validation, and API documentation via Scribe. Included security measures cover rate limiting, headers, and content-type enforcement. It also offers localization, testing, and static analysis tools for streamlined API development.
Content:
Kit by Steve Mc Dougallis is a Laravel API starter kit built around token-based authentication, API documentation, and security-first defaults. It targets developers who want a structured starting point for building versioned JSON APIs without wiring everything up from scratch.

Getting Started: Clone the repository, install dependencies, and run the setup script:
```
composer install
composer run setup
php artisan serve
```
The `composer run setup` command copies `.env.example` to `.env`, generates an app key, and runs migrations against a local SQLite database.

API Architecture: Kit takes a few deliberate architectural positions:
- No global/api prefix— routes are versioned directly, e.g., `/v1/auth/login`.
- Invokable controllers only— each controller has a single `__invoke` method.
- Form Requests for validation— request payloads are validated in dedicated Form Request classes, with DTO-style payload classes in `app/Http/Payloads/V1`.
- JSON:API resource format— responses follow a consistent structure for entity data.

Authentication: Nine routes handle the full auth lifecycle:
- Registration and login (both return tokens via Laravel Sanctum).
- `/v1/auth/me` (requires Bearer token).
- Email verification via signed URLs.
- Password reset with anti-enumeration responses (the same response is returned whether or not the email exists).

Security Defaults: Kit includes several security defaults out of the box:
- ULID primary keys for user records.
- Predefined rate limits on auth endpoints, configurable in the App Service Provider.
- Mandatory Content-Type: application/json enforcement on write requests.
- Hardened response headers, including `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, and `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer`.
- Request ID tracking.
- Audit logging for sensitive operations.

Sunset Middleware: Kit also includes a Sunset middleware for deprecating API endpoints over time. It is applied directly to a route with three parameters: the sunset date, a successor URL, and a boolean to control enforcement. For example:
```
Route::middleware('sunset:2027-01-01,https://api.acme.com/v2/auth/login,true')
    ->post('/v1/auth/login', LoginController::class);
```
While the endpoint is active, the middleware appends Deprecation, Sunset, and Link headers to responses, allowing clients to detect deprecation and plan accordingly. After the sunset date and enforcement, the endpoint returns a 410 Gone status.

Documentation: API documentation is generated using Scribe. Annotations are attribute-based rather than docblock-based, and the setup produces an OpenAPI specification alongside the docs.

Localization: The kit respects the `Accept-Language` request header and responds with a `Content-Language` header. Supported locales default to English, with translation files at `lang/en/api.php` and `lang/es/api.php`.

Tooling: The project uses Pest for tests, PHPStan for static analysis, Pint for code formatting, and Rector for automated refactoring. GitHub Actions workflows are included.

Title: Ward: A Security Scanner for Laravel
Author: Yannick Lyn Fatt
Summary: Ward is a Go-based command-line security scanner for Laravel projects. It analyzes project structure, environment variables, configurations, dependencies, and applies 42 security rules across categories like secrets, injection, and XSS. It offers detailed reports in multiple formats and can be integrated into CI/CD pipelines with configurable severity thresholds. Installation requires Go, and usage involves initializing and scanning local or remote projects.
Content:
Ward is a command-line security scanner for Laravel, created by El Jakani Yassine. Written in Go, it is designed around Laravel's structure. Instead of running generic pattern matching across the codebase, Ward first parses the project's structure—including routes, models, controllers, middleware, Blade templates, config files, environment variables, and dependencies—and then runs targeted checks against that context.

Installation requires Go. After installing, run:

```
go install github.com/eljakani/ward@latest
```

Ensure that `$GOPATH/bin` is in your PATH:

```
export PATH="$PATH:$(goenv GOPATH)/bin"
```

Once installed, initialize Ward with:

```
ward init
```

This creates `~/.ward/` with a default configuration file, over 42 built-in rules organized by category, and directories for reports and scan history.

To scan a project, point Ward at a local directory:

```
ward scan /path/to/laravel-project
```

Or at a remote Git repository:

```
ward scan https://github.com/user/laravel-project.git
```

During a scan, Ward displays a terminal user interface (TUI) showing progress and live severity counts. After completion, it presents a sortable findings table with severity badges, category grouping, and a detail panel with descriptions, code snippets, and remediation guidance.

Ward includes four independent scan engines:

- **env-scanner:** Checks 8 items in your `.env` file, such as debug mode enabled in production, missing or weak `APP_KEY`, and secrets leaked in `.env.example`.
- **config-scanner:** Runs 13 checks across `config/*.php` files, including hardcoded credentials, insecure session flags, CORS wildcard origins, and missing security options.
- **dependency-scanner:** Queries the OSV.dev advisory database in real time against your `composer.lock` to identify vulnerable Packagist packages.
- **rules-scanner:** Applies 42 rules across seven categories, including secrets (hardcoded passwords, API keys), injection (SQL, command, eval), XSS (unescaped Blade output, JavaScript injection), debug artifacts (`dd()`, `dump()`, `phpinfo()`), weak cryptography (MD5, SHA1, insecure RNG), configuration issues (CORS, CSRF, mass assignment), and authentication gaps (missing middleware, absent rate limiting).

Output formats are configurable in `~/.ward/config.yaml`:

- **json:** Machine-readable results
- **sarif:** Compatible with GitHub Code Scanning and IDE integrations
- **html:** Standalone dark-themed visual report
- **markdown:** Suitable for pull request comments

Ward can be integrated into CI/CD pipelines. It returns non-zero exit codes when findings meet or exceed specified severity levels, enabling deployment gating:

```
ward scan --output json --fail-on high
```

Title: Polyscope Is an Ai-First Dev Environment for Orchestrating Agents
Author: Paul Redmond
Summary: Beyond Code has launched Polyscope, a free AI-first development environment for orchestrating multiple AI agents simultaneously. Key features include parallel agent execution, fast copy-on-write cloning, a built-in preview browser, multi-model querying, and workspace integration. Available for macOS, Polyscope offers free plans for solo developers and teams.
Content:
Polyscope Is an AI-First Dev Environment for Orchestrating Agents

The team at Beyond Code has announced Polyscope, a free agent orchestration tool designed to run multiple AI agents simultaneously. 

From the announcement: "Today we're announcing Polyscope - the free agent orchestration tool of my dreams. Run dozens of AI agents at the same time, blazing fast copy-on-write clones, a built-in preview browser you can use to visually prompt your agents, and much more."

Key features include:
- Running dozens of AI agents in parallel
- Copy-on-write clones for fast agent branching
- Built-in preview browser for visually prompting agents
- Querying multiple models to collaborate on a question
- Connecting workspaces to multiple repositories

Polyscope is available for macOS at getpolyscope.com, offering a free plan and options for solo developers and teams.

Title: Hit & Run / Assault - 400 Block Witney Avenue North
Author: Unknown
Summary: Police are investigating a civilian vehicle chase and assault on March 5, 2026, on Witney Avenue North. A vehicle chased and struck another, then an occupant assaulted the driver. The victim sustained non-life-threatening injuries. The suspect vehicle fled before police arrived. Both parties are believed to know each other; police continue their investigation.
Content:
Police are investigating following a civilian vehicle chase that led to an assault. Shortly before 10:00 a.m. on March 5, 2026, officers responded to the 400 block of Witney Avenue North after reports of altercations between the occupants of two vehicles. It was reported that a civilian vehicle had been chasing another before intentionally striking it in the 400 block of Witney Avenue. An occupant then exited the vehicle and began assaulting the driver of the struck vehicle. 

Upon arrival, officers found the assault victim suffering from non-life-threatening injuries; the other involved vehicle had fled prior to police arrival. Through investigation, officers learned that a confrontational exchange occurred in the area of 22nd Street West and Idylwyld Drive North between two parties before one began chasing the other in their vehicles. During the chase, multiple parked vehicles were struck and damaged. Both parties are believed to have known each other, and police continue their investigation.

Title: A Tale of Two Earnings Cycles
Author: Phil Mackintosh
Summary: In Q4 2025, both Nasdaq-100® large caps and S&P 600 small caps showed strong earnings growth, with the Nasdaq posting 17% year-over-year gains and small caps returning to positive growth after 11 quarters of declines. The drivers differ: large caps benefited from stable margins and less sensitivity to inflation and interest rate headwinds, while small caps' recovery was aided by easing inflation, wage pressures, and Fed rate cuts.
Content:
Earnings: A Tale of Two Earnings Cycles

The fourth quarter of 2025's earnings season continued a recent trend: the Nasdaq-100® large caps and S&P 600 small caps both experienced strong earnings growth. However, beyond the headline numbers, the drivers of these earnings trends are quite different.

Both large and small caps are showing strong earnings growth now. The Nasdaq-100® posted 17% year-over-year earnings growth in Q4 2025, extending its streak to 11 consecutive quarters of at least 15% growth. In contrast, small caps had experienced 11 straight quarters of negative earnings growth from Q3 2022 through Q1 2025. But the trend reversed in Q2 2025, and Q4 marks its third consecutive quarter of positive earnings growth.

Looking ahead, consensus estimates project the Nasdaq-100® to continue its streak of over 15% earnings growth for the next year, while small caps are expected to maintain their positive growth trend. Despite both indexes currently showing strong earnings growth, the underlying drivers differ significantly.

The Nasdaq-100® has been less sensitive to the headwinds that pushed small caps into recession. Earnings growth can be broken down into three components: earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT) growth, interest costs, and taxes and other items. The analysis uses last twelve months (LTM) data.

Small caps faced multiple challenges. Over the past couple of years, they experienced negative EBIT growth due to margin pressures from high inflation and wage increases, along with less pricing power compared to large caps. Rising interest costs, driven by Federal Reserve rate hikes from 2022 to 2024, also impacted small caps. Recently, as inflation and wage growth slowed, EBIT growth turned positive, aided by Fed rate cuts that stabilized interest costs, which are expected to decline further in upcoming quarters.

In contrast, the Nasdaq-100® appeared to operate in a world less affected by these headwinds. While these challenges impacted large caps less—thanks to their higher margins—they still played a role in their earnings dynamics.

Title: Traffic Collison - Circle Dr E/Hwy 11 North Bound Lanes
Author: Unknown
Summary: Saskatoon police report a semi-trailer collision on Highway 11 near Circle Drive, causing damage to the bridge. One eastbound lane on Circle Drive and one northbound lane on Highway 11 are now open. Traffic restrictions remain, with ongoing investigations and assessments. Motorists are advised to take alternate routes and expect delays. No injuries were reported.
Content:
The Saskatoon Police Service advises that City of Saskatoon engineers have assessed the damage, and one eastbound lane on Circle Drive and one northbound lane of Highway 11 have been opened for traffic to proceed.

The Saskatoon Police Service Collision Analyst Unit, in cooperation with the Saskatchewan Highway Patrol, is investigating the collision. No injuries were reported.

Traffic flow restrictions will remain until further notice. Motorists are asked to take alternate routes or expect delays in the area.

The police report that a semi-trailer carrying equipment on the northbound lane of Highway 11, entering the city from Regina at the Circle Drive overpass, was too high for the structure, causing significant damage.

As a result, the northbound lanes of Highway 11 into the city and the eastbound lanes of Circle Drive over the bridge are closed until engineers from the City of Saskatoon assess the damage and determine if it is safe for travel. Motorists are advised to take alternate routes until further notice.

Title: Sask. Party government under fire for $10-a-day child care cutoff
Author: Michael Joel-Hansen
Summary: Saskatchewan's new child care policy restricts $10-a-day coverage to children born after April 1, leaving families with children born before that date facing higher fees. Critics, including NDP Leader Carla Beck, and providers argue the cutoff was poorly communicated and unfair, impacting families like Tina Lloyd’s, whose daughter’s costs will rise significantly. The government defends the deal as the best possible negotiated outcome.
Content:
Saskatchewan NDP Leader Carla Beck criticized the provincial government after it announced that only children who turn six and are born after April 1 will qualify for the $10-a-day childcare rate, leaving some families facing full fees.

Tina Lloyd had been hopeful she’d have some monthly breathing room in her family’s budget when she heard that six-year-olds in their kindergarten year would be covered for $10-a-day child care under the upcoming agreement between the provincial and federal governments. The coverage was expected to last until the end of kindergarten. Lloyd, whose daughter has a March 31 birthday, and her husband are facing increased care fees, which will rise from $217 each month to $900 for full-time care or $650 for half-time care. The provincial government recently informed parents and providers that the agreement covers $10-daily child care only for children who turn six after April 1, when the new agreement takes effect.

Under the new provisions, children with birthdays before April 1 will not be covered. “Their notice this week said, ‘No, it’s going to be an extra $680 starting less than a month from now,’” Lloyd said.

When the $1.6 billion funding agreement was announced in November 2025, provincial Education Minister Everett Hindley stated that ensuring children did not age out of the program at six was a major priority. “If we’re going to renegotiate this deal, this is one thing we would certainly want to try to get in there,” he said at the time.

Speaking with media in Regina on Thursday, Hindley acknowledged the agreement is “not a perfect deal” but insisted it is an improvement over the previous arrangement. He explained that the deal was the result of negotiations between the provincial and federal governments, which involved compromises. “This is the best deal that we were able to obtain,” Hindley said.

Shelby Fox, owner of Early Bird Daycare in Warman, believed the province indicated the new child care agreement would extend $10-a-day childcare to all six-year-olds, which providers had been advocating for. Fox has one child in her care affected by the cutoff and described the situation as “disheartening.” To manage increased costs, Lloyd is considering picking up more nursing shifts, and she and her husband may also ask grandparents for help with child care.

The timing of the notice was also problematic. Providers learned about the April 1 cutoff only on Monday. Fox noted that provincial regulations require licensed providers to give at least one month’s notice before any fee changes, and she argued they did not receive sufficient notice based on these standards.

Title: Say Cheese! Meet SmashingConf Amsterdam 🇳🇱
Author: hello@smashingmagazine.com (Iris Lješnjanin)
Summary: The first Smashing Conf Amsterdam will be held April 13–16, 2026, at Pathé Tuschinski, focusing on UX, design systems, accessibility, AI, and modern CSS. The conference emphasizes live workflows and practical insights for designers and UI engineers. Tickets are €724.79 in person and €200 online, with discounts for Smashing Membership.
Content:
Events, Smashing Conf

About The Author

Juggling between three languages on a daily basis, Iris is known for her love of linguistics, arts, web design, and typography.

Iris ↬ Email Newsletter

We’ve been passionate about design & UX for years. We’ve published articles on design systems and usability, inclusive design and product design, UX research, and enterprise UX. Now it’s time to bring it all together: in-person, in a new location, with a new spirit of curiosity and community. And it’s happening this April!

“Gezelligheid” Meets Modern Tech

One of the most difficult Dutch words to translate directly into English is Gezelligheid (pronounced guh-ZELL-uhk-hyde). It’s a foundational concept in Dutch culture that loosely translates to coziness, conviviality, and a warm, social, or inviting atmosphere—and that’s exactly what Smashing Conf Amsterdam has to offer.

What Should You Expect?

Since launching our first Smashing Conf in 2012, each event has focused on how industry professionals work, fail, and succeed. We encourage speakers to share lessons learned and demonstrate their processes. Don’t be surprised to see speakers sitting down and showing their design process or setting up a Figma board and designing live with the audience.

Unlike traditional conferences that rely on polished slide decks, Smashing Conf emphasizes a “live-work” philosophy. Speakers are encouraged to showcase their actual workflows—coding live, designing in Figma, and troubleshooting in real-time—to provide actionable insights rather than high-level theory.

Previous conference talks are available on our YouTube channel.

Title: The SIP Formula Is Broken and Everyone Knows It
Author: Phil Mackintosh
Summary: The SIP (Securities Information Processor) and NBBO (National Best Bid and Offer) were designed to protect investors and improve liquidity, but the current market structure exploits economic incentives without adding value. The SIP's revenue-sharing formula favors data providers and less-liquid stocks, with quotes playing a more significant role than trades in price discovery, raising concerns about its effectiveness.
Content:
Market Infrastructure: The SIP Formula Is Broken and Everyone Knows It

February 26, 2026 — 05:20 pm EST

Investors around the world know that it's important to understand the right prices for stocks and ideally avoid paying too much for the stocks they buy. In the U.S., solving that problem included creating a consolidated tape (also known as Securities Information Processor, or SIP) and a National Best Bid and Offer (NBBO) as part of Reg NMS.

The SIP has been undeniably beneficial for the public. By consolidating all the best prices, it protected investors, reduced trading costs and complexity, and arguably improved liquidity, helping attract more companies to U.S. public markets. However, the NBBO isn’t a “free service.” It results from venues and traders who decide to advertise their liquidity publicly. Regulators at the time recognized that those providing data to the public should be rewarded and incentivized.

Today, the market has evolved in ways that exploit economic incentives—often without adding the intended value to the public, who expect to pay for reliable NBBO quotes. The SIP formula works to reward those providing its data. As detailed elsewhere, the SIP charges different types of users different prices based on how much that data benefits them. Professionals pay more than retail investors, and electronic trading systems pay more than individual traders. It also rewards data contributors to the SIP. Since quotes and trades both contain signals about a stock’s value and market interest, the current formula splits revenue evenly: 50% to trades and 50% to quotes, with more money allocated to less-liquid stocks that require market makers.

The totals are shown in Chart 1. Note that “non-display” data, used for routing, trade pricing, and benchmarking—rather than on-screen display—relies on the “use” of data by computers, rather than for broker or customer screens, suggesting quotes are twice as important as trades. A number of well-known academics have studied price discovery extensively. Their research indicates that, for most stocks, quotes matter about twice as much as trades. This makes sense, considering that many trades use NBBO prices—such as midpoint trades that occur at the NBBO midpoint at the time.

The more detailed analysis from Brogaard’s study supports this view.

Title: Now Shipping: Accessible UX Research, A New Smashing Book By Michele Williams
Author: hello@smashingmagazine.com (Ari Stiles)
Summary: The Smashing Book “Accessible UX Research” by Michele Williams is now available worldwide. It offers practical guidance on inclusive UX research, focusing on assistive technologies, diverse disabilities, and integrating accessibility into the design process. The book provides strategies for involving disabled users, managing resources, and enhancing research quality across various professional roles.
Content:
The newest Smashing Book, “Accessible UX Research” by Michele Williams, is now shipping worldwide. This book focuses on research, assistive technology, different types of disability, and integrating accessibility into the entire design process. It aims to help readers make their UX research more inclusive and thorough, regardless of budget or timeline.

Good UX research is fundamental to creating great products. It reduces guesswork and helps solve problems early. One effective way to enhance research is by ensuring inclusivity—testing with users with diverse needs and abilities and using their feedback to develop more accessible products.

Accessible UX Research provides a clear, easy-to-follow roadmap for planning and executing effective user research. Dr. Michele Williams draws on extensive experience to offer guidance suitable for various professionals interested in digital product development. 

For beginners, the book offers tips on making research more inclusive, asking better questions, understanding biases, and managing limited resources effectively. Accessibility professionals will find detailed information on assistive technologies, how to incorporate them into testing, and ways to document and share results to impact product development. Developers, managers, and others interested in understanding how different abilities affect user experience will benefit from historical context, descriptions, and cultural insights to better interpret accessibility recommendations.

The book is a practical guide to inclusive UX research, not a checklist. It provides strategies for including disabled participants and ensuring research is effective and reliable.

Title: Filament v5.3.0 Released with Deferred Tab Badges and Column Manager Improvements
Author: Paul Redmond
Summary: Filament v5.3.0 adds ten features, including deferred tab badge loading, modal/slideover column manager, customizable Rich Editor file attachments, and sort loading indicators. It expands rate limiting options, enhances navigation group definitions with PHP enums, and introduces a Callout controls slot. Bug fixes address sidebar state restoration and missing chart translations.
Content:
Filament v5.3.0 introduces ten new features, including deferred tab badge loading, column manager modal and slideover support, enhancements to Rich Editor plugins, and sort loading indicators on table columns.

Deferred tab badge loading allows badge counts to load independently from the tab content, improving performance by avoiding unnecessary database queries when badge values are expensive to compute.

The column manager now supports displaying in a modal or slideover, providing additional layout options beyond the default dropdown for managing table column visibility.

Rich Editor plugins can now supply their own file attachment providers, enabling plugin authors to integrate custom upload and storage behaviors directly into the editor without external configuration. Additionally, plugins can control the visibility and state of toolbar buttons for more granular editing control.

Table columns now display a loading spinner on sort icons during sorting operations, offering visual feedback when sorting takes a moment to complete.

Rate limiting configurations have been expanded to support user ID and email address in addition to IP-based options. Navigation groups for pages and resources now accept Unit Enum values, simplifying the definition of navigation groups using PHP enums.

The Callout component has gained a controls slot, allowing custom action buttons or content alongside callout messages. Resources now support additional configuration options, and the component color palette has been extended with more Tailwind CSS color options.

Bug fixes include correct restoration of sidebar state on desktop page loads and the addition of missing chart widget translations.

Title: Evade Police/Impaired Driving - McPherson Ave, Preston Ave.
Author: Unknown
Summary: A 41-year-old Saskatoon man faces multiple charges after evading police overnight. The incident began when police attempted to stop a suspicious black Chrysler 200, which fled, leading to reckless driving and a crash. The male suspect, wanted on warrants and suspected of methamphetamine influence, was arrested after fleeing on foot. He is charged with impaired driving, dangerous driving, evading police, and related offenses.
Content:
A 41-year-old Saskatoon man is facing multiple charges following an incident in which a driver evaded police overnight.

At approximately 1:00 a.m., March 4, 2026, an officer on patrol spotted a suspicious vehicle in the 500 block of 8th Street East. The officer attempted to stop a black Chrysler 200 when it turned onto McPherson Avenue. The driver was slow to pull over, and when he did, a male and a female exited the vehicle and fled on foot. The driver then sped away as the officer approached the vehicle.

The suspect vehicle evaded police, driving recklessly in the area of Wilson Crescent and Preston Avenue. The driver attempted to overtake a snowplow; however, his tires had been damaged earlier in the incident, causing him to lose control and end up on the median. The male suspect tried to flee on foot but was taken into custody.

He was found to be wanted on several warrants and is believed to have been under the influence of methamphetamine at the time. The man is now charged with possession of bear spray, impaired driving, dangerous driving, evading police, and breaching court conditions.

Title: Update - Additional Arrest - Homicide - 1700 Block 20th Street West
Author: Unknown
Summary: A second male, aged 26, has been charged with aggravated assault and manslaughter in connection with the February 3, 2026, homicide and will appear in court. An 18-year-old previously charged with manslaughter was arrested on February 26 and now faces new charges including accessory after the fact, public mischief, and obstruction of justice, with manslaughter and assault charges withdrawn. The investigation is ongoing.
Content:
A second male has been charged in connection to a February 3, 2026, homicide. A 26-year-old male is charged with aggravated assault and manslaughter. He will appear in court this afternoon.

A man previously charged in the homicide investigation is now facing new charges. On February 26, 2026, investigators attended a correctional facility in Saskatoon and arrested an 18-year-old man who was previously charged with manslaughter in relation to the February 3rd homicide. He is now charged with accessory after the fact to manslaughter, accessory after the fact to aggravated assault, public mischief, and obstruction of justice. The charges of manslaughter and aggravated assault have been withdrawn.

The Major Crime Unit continues to investigate.
